summary: Matched the lbHeap report function `lbHeap_80015DF8` by preserving an apparently unused extra vararg in an `OSReport` call. The format string only prints `p->size / 1024`, but passing `p->size` as an additional argument reproduces target codegen and takes the function from 99.87654% to 100.0% while keeping function size 324/324 bytes.
what_changed: In `src/melee/lb/lbheap.c`, updated the final per-block `OSReport` inside `lbHeap_80015DF8` from `OSReport(" / %5d KB\n", p->size / 1024);` to `OSReport(" / %5d KB\n", p->size / 1024, p->size);`.
The added `p->size` argument is not consumed by the format string, but was retained because it matches the target binary's vararg calling pattern.
Reported matching result for `main/melee/lb/lbheap`: `lbHeap_80015DF8` improved from 99.87654% to 100.0%.
Function size remained unchanged at 324/324 bytes.
smart_moves: Recognized that matching required modeling the original call signature/codegen rather than only the logical behavior implied by the format string.
Kept an extra vararg to `OSReport` even though it is semantically unused by the printed string, preserving ABI-level/codegen evidence from the target.
Verified the minimal one-line change with both object build and objdiff, then ran `melee-issues` to check that no issues were introduced in `src/melee/lb/lbheap.c`.
decomp_lessons: For variadic functions like `OSReport`, unused extra arguments can still be necessary for an exact match because the compiler emits argument setup even if the format string does not consume them.
Do not remove or omit suspicious-looking extra varargs solely because they appear redundant at the C semantic level; compare against target codegen first.
Small decomp fixes can hinge on reproducing call-site argument evaluation rather than changing control flow, data structures, or formatting strings.
When matching report/debug functions, preserve target quirks in `OSReport` calls, including excess arguments, if they affect generated assembly.
naming_conventions: Existing heap globals retained: `lbHeap_80431FA0`, with `arena_hi` and `arena_lo` referenced after the changed loop.
No new symbols or renames were introduced in this PR.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use extra arguments to variadic calls as a matching tactic when target assembly shows additional argument setup.
The target codegen apparently evaluates/passes both `p->size / 1024` and raw `p->size` for the final `OSReport`, despite the format string only consuming the KB value.
Ran `DYLD_LIBRARY_PATH=/Library/Developer/CommandLineTools/usr/lib cargo run -p melee-issues`; reported 117 pre-existing issues elsewhere and none from `src/melee/lb/lbheap.c`.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR body explicitly documents the key rationale: the extra `p->size` vararg is consumed by codegen but not by the format string, and keeping it matches the target.
follow_up_queries: Find other `OSReport` or variadic callsites where unused extra arguments may be needed for matching.
Search for unmatched lbHeap functions with near-100% objdiff scores where argument setup differs from source semantics.
Audit report/debug print functions for format strings that consume fewer arguments than are passed in target codegen.
